value: INC Ransom (aka GOLD IONIC; MITRE ATT&CK G1032) listed City of Acworth GA (acworth-ga.gov) on its Tor leak site on 2026-07-02; the city disclosed the underlying intrusion began around 2026-06-08 and stated systems were restored with no disruption to day-to-day operations.

City of Oak Park MI (oakparkmi.gov) was added to the same INC Ransom leak site in the same posting window as Acworth, consistent with an opportunistic multi-victim spree rather than a single bespoke campaign against Acworth alone.

context: File extension appended to files encrypted by INC Ransomware (MITRE S1139); documented group behavior
confidence: not independently confirmed for the Acworth encryptor build.

context: Plaintext ransom note dropped in encrypted directories by INC Ransomware; a matching INC-README.html copy is also written and pushed to reachable network printers as hard-copy extortion notices.
Scheduled task name used by INC Ransom operators for persistence of staged tooling ahead of the encryption run.
PsExec (MITRE S0029) renamed to winupd.exe to masquerade as a legitimate Windows update binary before being installed as a demand-start LocalSystem service (T1569.002).
Native Windows LOLBin abused by INC Ransom operators to disable Windows Defender ahead of encryption (T1685).
SoftPerfect Network Scanner binary used by INC Ransom for internal network-service discovery (T1046).
PowerShell credential-dumper variant (HackTool.PS1.VeeamCreds) targeting Veeam Backup & Replication credential stores
confidence: reported in recent INC Ransom incident telemetry; not independently confirmed for the Acworth case.

context: Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway unauthenticated RCE documented in INC Ransom's/GOLD IONIC's general initial-access playbook (SentinelOne, SOCRadar). NOT confirmed as the entry vector for the City of Acworth incident specifically -- no technical root-cause has been published for this case.
Fortinet FortiClient EMS SQL-injection-to-RCE documented in the group's general initial-access playbook. NOT confirmed as the entry vector for the City of Acworth incident specifically.

value: INC Ransom exfiltrates staged 7-Zip/WinRAR archives to cloud storage via MegaSync (T1537) prior to detonating the encryptor; Rclone has also been observed for alternative-protocol exfiltration in related incidents.

Cross-platform Rust-rewritten INC encryptor builds target both Windows (PE64, sometimes VMProtect-packed) and Linux/ESXi hosts, and use partial encryption with multi-threading to speed the encryption run (T1486).

Delaware County, PA disclosed unauthorized network activity beginning 2026-06-26 disrupting courthouse, library and correctional-facility systems; the county has declined to confirm whether the incident involved ransomware as of 2026-07-15, illustrating the disclosure-silence pattern common to civic-sector intrusions in this same window.
